name: Razorpay-Style Async Payment Gateway
scenario: Build an async payment gateway using CompletableFuture pipelines. Each payment goes through: card validation → bank authorization → fraud check (parallel with auth) → ledger update → notification. The pipeline is fully non-blocking. Failures at any stage produce graceful degradation with logged errors and status tracking.
description: A full async payment processing pipeline using CompletableFuture chains, thenCompose for sequential steps, allOf for parallel steps, exceptionally for error handling, and ConcurrentHashMap + AtomicInteger for concurrent state tracking.

core.description: `Payment` record: paymentId, cardNumber, amount, currency. `PaymentResult` record: paymentId, status (AUTHORIZED/DECLINED/FAILED), message.

Async pipeline methods as CompletableFuture chains:
- `validateCard(Payment)` → CF<Payment>: 100ms, fail if amount ≤ 0
- `authorizeWithBank(Payment)` → CF<Payment>: 200ms, 10% random decline
- `checkFraud(Payment)` → CF<Boolean>: 150ms, 5% flag as fraud
- `updateLedger(Payment)` → CF<String>: 80ms, returns txnId
- `sendNotification(PaymentResult)` → CF<Void>: 50ms, fire-and-forget

`processPayment(Payment)` method: thenCompose chain validate → allOf(authorize, fraud-check) → updateLedger → sendNotification. Fraud check and authorization run IN PARALLEL using allOf. Total pipeline: ~350ms vs ~580ms sequential.

Error handling: exceptionally on each step with appropriate fallback. Declined payments return DECLINED status. Fraud-flagged payments are logged but not necessarily blocked (just logged). Failed services return FAILED status.
`ConcurrentHashMap<String, String> paymentStatuses` and `AtomicInteger` counters for: total, authorized, declined, failed.
Process 5 payments concurrently. Print individual results as they complete, final summary at end.
ExecutorService provided as custom pool (not default ForkJoinPool). Proper shutdown.

bonus.description: Add timeout: each payment pipeline has a 1-second total timeout using `orTimeout(1, SECONDS)` (Java 9+) or `completeOnTimeout(defaultResult, 1, SECONDS)`. Timed-out payments counted as FAILED.
Add retry: if bank authorization fails with a transient error (not card declined), retry up to 2 times with 200ms delay. Use CompletableFuture recursion for retry.
Add `CompletableFuture<PaymentReport> generateReport()` that completes when ALL payments are done. Uses allOf, produces a sorted summary by status.

data_to_use:
Payments:
- PAY-001: card-4242, ₹1500 — valid
- PAY-002: card-4343, ₹2000 — valid
- PAY-003: card-invalid, ₹0 — validation fails
- PAY-004: card-5555, ₹5000 — valid (may be declined randomly)
- PAY-005: card-6666, ₹800 — valid
example_output:
=== Razorpay Async Payment Gateway ===
Processing 5 payments in parallel...

PAY-003: FAILED — invalid amount (₹0)
PAY-001: AUTHORIZED — TXN-001 ✓
PAY-005: AUTHORIZED — TXN-005 ✓
PAY-002: AUTHORIZED — TXN-002 ✓
PAY-004: DECLINED — insufficient funds

=== Summary ===
Total:      5
Authorized: 3
Declined:   1
Failed:     1
Pipeline time: ~350ms (sequential: ~580ms)

criteria: Results complete out of order (5 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Module 10 capstone — no AI. All patterns covered in Topics 10.1-10.5. Key design: processPayment returns CF<PaymentResult>. allOf(authCF, fraudCF) wraps parallel steps, thenCompose to updateLedger. whenComplete updates ConcurrentHashMap + AtomicInteger. Main: list of CFs, allOf to wait, thenRun to print summary.
submission_instructions: Save as PaymentGateway.java. Push to GitHub with commit message: 'Module 10 capstone: async payment gateway'. This is the most production-like code in the course so far.
tips_for_success: allOf returns CF<Void> — chain thenCompose from it by using the captured auth/fraud futures
whenComplete on processPayment final stage updates ConcurrentHashMap and AtomicInteger
All 5 processPayment calls happen before any join() — true parallel fan-out
